title: marketing — subprocessors
description: The Marketing module engages NINE third-party subprocessors — one per ad-network provider driver. Every subprocessor receives hashed PII (email SHA256, phone SHA256, name SHA256) via HTTPS. GDPR Art. 28 DPA references + Art. 30 records of processing activities. Every tenant that activates a provider effectively adds that subprocessor to their processing register; the tenant admin acknowledges the DPA reference before configuring.

subprocessors.purpose: Server-side conversion event forwarding via the Meta Conversions API. Enables ad-spend optimization on Meta (Facebook + Instagram) properties.
subprocessors.data_hashing: SHA256 of lowercased+trimmed email; SHA256 of E.164 phone digits-only
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges the DPA before saving the provider config

subprocessors.purpose: Enhanced Conversions upload for Google Ads campaigns. Powers offline conversion attribution.
subprocessors.data_hashing: SHA256 of lowercased email; provider prefers hashed but accepts raw (driver always hashes)

subprocessors.purpose: Server-side event capture via GA4 Measurement Protocol. Feeds analytics dashboards + audience building.
subprocessors.data_hashing: none required; client_id + user_id passed as-is (identifiers, not raw PII)

subprocessors.purpose: Server-to-server delivery to a tenant-hosted GTM Server container. The container itself may fan out to further destinations (this is the tenant's decision, out of Stackra's scope).
data_categories: caller-configured schema — Stackra delivers the canonical event shape + attribution + consent snapshot
subprocessors.data_hashing: none by Stackra; the GTM container may hash on its own
subprocessors.dpa_url: https://privacy.google.com/businesses/processorterms/ (base GTM DPA — tenant additionally responsible for their container's downstream)
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin acknowledges DPA + confirms the container URL

subprocessors.purpose: Server-side conversion tracking via TikTok Events API. Powers TikTok ad campaign optimization.
Server-side conversion events for LinkedIn Insight Tag. Powers LinkedIn ad campaign optimization (B2B focus).
Server-side conversion events via Snapchat Conversions API.
Server-side conversion events via Pinterest Conversions API.
Generic HMAC-signed JSON webhook. Escape hatch for tenants using Segment / Rudderstack / custom analytics warehouses. Data ultimately delivered to a caller-configured URL.
data_categories: full marketing_event row + attribution snapshot + consent snapshot
subprocessors.data_hashing: none by Stackra — caller's endpoint responsible for handling
subprocessors.dpa_url: N/A — the tenant is responsible for their downstream endpoint's DPA if any
subprocessors.activation_gate: tenant admin confirms URL + acknowledges their own DPA responsibility

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching MarketingProviderCircuitBreakerOpenedNotification / MarketingDeadLetterCreatedNotification / MarketingProviderTestFailedNotification / MarketingRoasReportReadyNotification / MarketingConsentSuppressionSpikeNotification / MarketingHighFailureRateNotification / MarketingConfigurationChangedNotification via mail / SMS / push / Slack channels.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.reference: growth::attribution owns UTM + click-ID capture; marketing reads via AttributionResolver injection. attribution's subprocessors.json documents cookie-related processing.
compliance::encryption owns the encryption service used for at-rest encryption of marketing_provider_configs.config + marketing_deliveries.request_payload.

notes: This file exists deliberately with 9 active subprocessors. Every tenant that configures a marketing provider effectively activates that subprocessor for their tenant. The activation flow prompts the tenant admin to acknowledge the DPA reference before saving.
The tenant-facing subprocessor list (compliance/subprocessors surface) reads from this file + shows only the active subset per tenant (based on which providers the tenant has configured).
Adding a new provider driver = adding a new entry here + updating the tenant-facing subprocessor list + updating compliance.json Art. 28 evidence.
